template.name: ANSI A92.2 Major Structural Load Test

questions.prompt: Name of entity performing test documented.
Make, model, and serial number of aerial device documented.
Electrical Category documented.
Rated Line Voltage documented.
Test type designated: Qualification or Periodic Test.
Date of test documented.
Signature/name of person performing test documented.
Equipment service age verified (15 years for first test, then every 10 years thereafter).
Structure inspected for unusual deformations.
Structure inspected for broken members.
Structure inspected for cracked welds.
All critical fasteners properly secured and torqued.
Equipment in suitable condition to proceed with load testing.
Vehicle meets manufacturer's minimum vehicle specifications.
Readily removable tools and material removed from platform.
If outriggers/stabilizers required: deployed per manufacturer's instructions.
Oscillating axle locks engaged (if equipped and required).
Parking brake set (if required).

sections.title: Level Surface Stability Test (1.5x Rated Capacity)

questions.prompt: Test conducted on firm, level surface.
Test load is 1.5x rated load capacity (platform capacity + supplemental capacity if applicable).
Load applied at center of platform.
Platform tested in every position within specific configuration.
Over center units: tested in over center position.
Unit remains stable with no instability observed.
No permanent deformation of any component observed.

sections.title: Slope Stability Test (1.33x Rated Capacity at 5° Slope)

questions.prompt: Test conducted on 5-degree slope (or manufacturer's maximum allowable slope if greater).
Slope in direction of least stability.
Test load is 1.33x rated load capacity (platform capacity + supplemental capacity if applicable).
Load applied at center of platform.
Platform tested in every position within specific configuration on slope.
Unit remains stable with no instability observed.
No permanent deformation of any component observed.
After load removal: no permanent deformations present.
After load removal: no new cracks in structural members or welds.
After load removal: no broken or damaged components.
Hydraulic/pneumatic systems operate normally after test.
Electrical/control systems operate normally after test.
Structure inspected for unusual deformations (per ANSI 8.2.5.2).
All damaged items replaced or repaired before return to service.
Does equipment meet all structural load test requirements?

fields.label: Equipment Service Age
fields.unit_hint: years (15 yr first test, then every 10 yr)

fields.label: Date of Last Major Structural Test
